---
title: "Evolution of Top Names"
author: ""
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: pygments
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 12,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

## 1. Load Libraries

```{r load-libraries}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```

## 2. Load and Explore Data

```{r load-data}
# Read the CSV file
data <- read_csv2("../data.csv")

# Display column names
colnames(data)
```

```{r explore-data}
# Display first few rows
head(data)

# Display data structure
str(data)

# Display summary statistics
summary(data)
```

## 3. Data Cleaning and Preparation

```{r clean-data}
# Clean and transform the data
data_clean <- data %>%
  # Convert birth year to numeric
  mutate(annais = as.numeric(as.character(annais))) %>%
  # Remove rows with missing birth years
  filter(!is.na(annais)) %>%
  # Remove rare names category
  filter(preusuel != "_PRENOMS_RARES") %>%
  # Create decade grouping
  mutate(decade = (annais %/% 10) * 10)

# Display cleaned data
head(data_clean)
```

## 4. Aggregate Data by Decade and Name

```{r aggregate-data}
# Aggregate births by decade and name
data_agg <- data_clean %>%
  group_by(decade, preusuel) %>%
  summarise(
    total = sum(nombre, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

head(data_agg)
```

## 5. Identify Top 10 Names

```{r top-names}
# Calculate total births for each name across all decades
top_names <- data_agg %>%
  group_by(preusuel) %>%
  summarise(total_all = sum(total)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_all)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 50)

# Display top 10 names
head(top_names, 50)
```

## 6. Filter Data for Top Names

```{r filter-top}
# Filter aggregated data to include only top 100 names
data_top <- data_agg %>%
  filter(preusuel %in% top_names$preusuel)
```

## 7. Visualization: Evolution of Top Names

```{r plot-evolution, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
# Create line plot showing evolution of top names over decades
ggplot(data_top, aes(x = decade, y = total, fill = preusuel)) +
  geom_area(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Top 100 Names by Decade",
    x = "Decade",
    y = "Number of Births",
    color = "Name"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(.~preusuel) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

## 8. Alternative Visualization: Top 10 Names Only

```{r plot-top-10, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
# Filter to show only top 10 names for clearer visualization
top_10_names <- top_names %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(preusuel)

data_top_10 <- data_top %>%
  filter(preusuel %in% top_10_names)

# Create line plot for top 10 names
ggplot(data_top_10, aes(x = decade, y = total, color = preusuel)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Top 10 Names by Decade",
    x = "Decade",
    y = "Number of Births",
    color = "Name"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

## 9. Summary Statistics

```{r summary-stats}
# Summary of the analysis
cat("Total unique names in dataset:", n_distinct(data_clean$preusuel), "\n")
cat("Time period covered:", min(data_clean$annais), "-", max(data_clean$annais), "\n")
cat("Total births recorded:", sum(data_clean$nombre, na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")

# Top 10 names by total births
cat("Top 10 Most Popular Names (All Time):\n")
print(head(top_names, 10))
```
